question: Implement a `Counter` class with a private integer `value`. Overload the pre-increment (`++`) operator to increment the `value` and return the incremented object. Also, overload the post-increment (`value++`) operator.

test_cases.expected:
Initial value: 5
After pre-increment: 6
After post-increment (original object): 7
Value returned by post-increment: UNDEFINED
--- New Counter x ---
Initial x value: 10
x after x++: 11
y (from x++): UNDEFINED
x after ++x: 12

errors: The post-increment operator `operator++(int)` returns a reference to a local temporary object (`temp`). This leads to a dangling reference and undefined behavior when the returned reference is used.
